content.claim: The current education minister has stated that declining child demographics make the current selective gimnázium system less justifiable and has placed the gymnasium entrance-exam system on the government agenda for review, but has explicitly stated she is not initiating abolition, only review, of the system.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Statements are recent and ongoing; no final policy decision has been announced
Minister's political party (Tisza) represents a recent change from long-standing Fidesz government; political feasibility of change remains contested
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
